description: In an apartment kitchen, Monica walks through a doorway holding a notepad and a cordless phone with its antenna extended. She looks down at the pad and says, "Save 350."
Monica continues walking into the room, raising her head.
Joey sits at a round dining table eating. A woman in a grey t-shirt stands near the counter and asks Monica, "How did you make $17?"
Monica stops, smiling, and says, "Well, my financially challenged friends I split my money and I bought some shares of CHP and ZXY."
Joey gestures with his fork toward Monica and asks, "How come those?"
Monica answers, "Well, CHP because I used to have a crush on Eric Estrada."
Monica smiles broadly, shifting her gaze upward briefly.
Monica says, "And ZXY because I think it sounds sexy," holding the phone antenna up near her shoulder.
The woman in the grey t-shirt looks toward Monica.
The woman in grey asks, "What happened to MEG"
Monica gestures with the phone antenna and her free hand, saying, "MEG was good for me, but I dumped her. You know, my motto is get out before they go down."
Joey closes his eyes, lowers his head, and covers his face with his hand.
With his eyes still covered, Joey says, "That is so not my"
The woman in the grey t-shirt sits down at the table next to Joey. She rests her chin on her hand and looks at him. Joey continues looking down.
The purple front door opens and Phoebe enters, wearing a black sweater, patterned leggings, and a backpack. Joey and the woman in grey turn their heads toward her.
Phoebe closes the door and says, "Oh, hey." Joey asks, "How's the dog?" Phoebe replies, "Yeah, okay."
Phoebe walks toward the table, adjusting her backpack strap, and says, "I talked to the vet. People are so nice upstate."
Phoebe stops near the table and says, "Anyway, he said that the little fella is going to be okay and I can pick him up tomorrow."
The woman in grey says, "Oh, good." Phoebe continues, "Yeah, but he did have to have like"
Phoebe makes small hand gestures and says, "a bunch of stitches. And he said that only once in a blue moon does a dog's ear grow back. So"
Phoebe crosses the index and middle fingers on both hands and holds them up, saying, "still hoping."
Phoebe lowers her hands. The woman in the grey t-shirt pours a drink from a pitcher into a glass.
The woman in grey looks at Phoebe and asks, "Okay, so, Phoebe, now are you going to call your dad to let him know that his dog is okay?"
Phoebe sits on the arm of a couch and says, "I don't want to meet my father over the phone. What am I going to say? Like, hi, I'm Phoebe, the daughter you"
Phoebe gestures with her hand and continues, "abandoned? Oh, by the way, I broke your dog."
Joey points his spoon toward Phoebe.
Joey says, "Hey, Phoebe, if you want, I'll do it." Phoebe nods slightly and replies, "Okay,"
Phoebe stands up from the arm of the couch.
Phoebe steps toward Joey, points at him, and says, "listen, just don't say anything about me, okay?"
Joey nods in agreement. Phoebe turns away from him.
Phoebe suddenly snaps her body around, pointing sharply toward Monica on the couch, and says loudly, "Don't"
Monica leans back slightly, then lowers her arm and gestures with her phone antenna.
Monica says, "be too long with the phone."
Phoebe lowers her hand and nods slightly.
The woman in the grey t-shirt says, "She'll be a much better friend once the market closes." Joey pulls out the phone's antenna and phone dialing beeps are audible as he presses the keypad.
Joey presses the phone to his ear. Phoebe stands nearby with her hand near her mouth, biting her fingernail.
Joey lowers the phone and says, "It's a woman," Phoebe quickly responds, "so talk to her."
Joey raises the phone back to his ear, his facial muscles tensing. Phoebe crosses her arms over her chest.
Joey speaks in a noticeably lower, raspy voice, "Hello, Mrs."
Joey adds, "Buffay."
Joey keeps the phone to his ear, maintaining his tense facial expression while listening.
In the same raspy voice, Joey says, "I know where your dog is."
Joey listens briefly, his eyes shifting slightly.
Joey continues in the raspy voice, "I want you to know he'll be returned to you almost as good as new within"
Joey finishes, "24 hours. Goodbye."
Joey presses a button on the phone to hang up. He lowers the phone and his facial expression returns to its baseline. He looks at Phoebe.
The woman in the grey t-shirt sits with her chin resting on her hand, looking steadily at Joey.
The woman in grey asks, "Why the voice?" Joey shrugs slightly and replies, "Hard to say." A bass-heavy instrumental music cue begins.

The music continues as the scene changes to an exterior shot of a city skyline over a river at dusk. The setting then changes to the inside of an apartment kitchen, where Chandler stands by the counter holding a blue mug.
Chandler hands the mug to Ross, who is seated at a small table. Chandler says, "Your cappuccino, sir."
Ross accepts the mug and replies, "Thank you."
Ross tears open a small packet. Chandler does the same with another packet in his hands.
Ross says, "You know, I think this is much better than the coffee house." Chandler responds, "Absolutely."
Ross pours the powder from his packet into his mug and begins stirring the liquid rapidly with a spoon. Chandler watches him.
Ross stops stirring and leans his head forward, peering into his mug. Chandler also looks down at the contents of Ross's mug.
Ross looks up at Chandler and asks, "How come it's not mixing with the water?"
Chandler glances down at the packet he is holding and says, "Well, the package says you have to constantly keep it moving."
Chandler pantomimes a stirring motion with his hand and says, "Stir and drink, stir and drink. Never let it settle."
Chandler quickly and vigorously stirs the liquid in his own mug, then immediately raises the mug to his mouth and drinks while keeping the spoon inside.
In a kitchen, Ross sits on a stool while Chandler stands nearby, both drinking from mugs. Chandler stirs his drink with a spoon.
Joey walks into the living room, putting on a brown jacket over a blue shirt, and approaches Ross and Chandler. "Hey, this is ridiculous. I'll tell you what."
Joey continues, "After I get back from my niece's christening I'll go down to the coffee house with"
Joey gestures as he adds, "you and we'll all have a nice cup of coffee. All right, no problem."
Joey points his thumb at himself, saying, "Joey's there." Chandler smiles, claps his hands together, and says, "Okay."
Ross looks down and shakes his head.
Ross looks up and says, "No."
Chandler and Joey look at Ross. Ross shakes his head again.
Ross adds, "man."
Ross stands up from the stool, saying, "I don't want to have to have Joey with me every time I want a decent cup of coffee."
Ross steps closer to Chandler.
Ross points as he says, "And I don't want to spend the rest of my life drinking cappuccino with a K."
Ross gestures toward the door, continuing, "I'd say you and I go back down there and stand up to those guys." Joey puts his hands on his hips.
Chandler smiles and raises one hand, saying, "All right, hang on a second there, Custer."
Joey turns to Ross, gesturing with his hands.
Joey asks, "Yeah, really? Ross, have you ever been beaten up before?" Ross nods, replying, "Yeah, sure."
Joey asks, "By someone besides Monica?" Ross lowers his gaze and answers, "No."
Ross turns his attention back to Chandler, raising his hands.
Ross steps forward, gesturing openly. "So what? So what if we get beaten up?"
Ross continues, "Maybe that's just something every man has to go through once in his life, like a rite of passage"
Chandler leans on the counter and says, "or something. Well, couldn't we just lose our virginities"
Chandler points to himself.
Chandler continues, "again, you know? Because I think actually mine's growing back," holding his hand to his chest.
Joey frowns at Chandler. Ross stares at Chandler.
Ross asks firmly, "All right, are you with me or not? Come on," walking toward the door. Chandler nods and says, "All right, I'm with you."
Chandler scratches behind his ear and says, "Just"
Chandler lowers his hand.
Chandler adds, "let me put on a clean pair of underwear for the hospital." He turns and walks out of the kitchen area.
Joey pats Ross on the shoulder as Ross opens the apartment door.

The setting changes to an apartment living room. Rachel, wearing a light beige cardigan over a black top and a plaid skirt, opens the door as Monica, wearing a black top with a white collar, rushes in, saying, "I need to borrow $100." Rachel asks, "What?"
Rachel closes the door and says, "Hi. Welcome home."
Monica walks quickly toward the kitchen area.
Monica turns to Rachel, saying, "I need to borrow $100." Rachel asks, "For what?"
Monica gestures emphatically and says, "I got to get back in the game."
Rachel asks, "Why? When did you get out of the game?"
Monica throws her hands down, saying, "Oh, no, I lost it all. Okay? I lost it." Rachel replies, "Oh, no."
Monica points at Rachel, saying, "Hey, I've come to terms with it. You have to, too."
Rachel looks at Monica, her hands raised slightly.
Rachel says, "Okay," lowering her hands.
Rachel turns away and starts walking toward the living room.
Rachel turns back to Monica, saying, "Look, Mon, I'm really sorry."
Monica asks quickly, "Where are we on the $100?"
Rachel places her hands on Monica's arms, saying, "I don don't have it." Monica replies, "But I need it," bringing her hands to her mouth.
Monica lowers her hands.
Monica says, "In other words, I'm going to have to take that horrible diner job"
Monica touches her own face.
Monica continues, "with the dancing and the costumes."
Monica drops to her knees, wrapping her arms around Rachel's legs and resting her head against Rachel's stomach.
Still holding onto Rachel's legs, Monica looks up and says, "I don't want to have to wear flame retardant boobs."
Rachel looks down at Monica with a concerned expression.
Rachel gently pats Monica's head and says, "Nobody does, honey."
The setting changes to the exterior of a house. Phoebe, wearing a dark green jacket, walks up the porch steps carrying a small dog wearing a protective cone collar and knocks on the front door.
A woman wearing a white top with a leopard print collar and large red hoop earrings opens the door and takes the dog from Phoebe, saying, "Hi, schnoodle. Oh, my God."
The woman asks, "What the hell happened to my dog?" Phoebe replies, "Well, it was an"
Phoebe gestures with her hands as she continues, "accident. And the woman who did this would never, ever hurt a dog on purpose. She's a vegetarian."
The woman examines the dog's head inside the cone.
The woman asks, "What are these, stitches?" Phoebe nods, replying, "Yeah, eight of them. And that's 56 to him," pointing at the dog.
The woman glares at Phoebe.
Phoebe points at the cone and adds, "Also, if it's raining, you can't let him look up too long, because that cone will fill up really, really fast."
The woman looks at Phoebe with an annoyed expression.
The woman says, "Thanks for bringing back what's left of him." Phoebe replies, "Sure."
Phoebe hesitates, then asks, "Is Frank home?"
The woman narrows her eyes and asks, "How do you know Frank?"
Phoebe shrugs slightly and answers, "Just from a long time ago. Is he here?"
The woman looks at Phoebe.
The woman nods slightly and says, "Yeah."
The woman turns her head toward the inside of the house.
The woman calls out, "Frank?" A young man's voice calls back, "Yeah."
A young man wearing an open white short-sleeve shirt over a white tank top walks into view at the doorway.
The young man asks, "What?"
Phoebe looks at the young man, appearing surprised.
Phoebe says, "Oh, okay."
Phoebe clarifies, "I mean, Frank SR."
The young man answers, "He went out for groceries." Phoebe asks, "Okay. So will he be back soon?"
The woman replies, "Well, he left four years ago, so we're expecting him back any minute now."
Phoebe nods slowly, her expression remaining serious.
Phoebe looks down briefly, then says, "I'm going to go. I'm sorry about the dog. Everything."
Phoebe repeats, "I'm sorry," and begins to back away from the door.
The young man watches as Phoebe turns and walks down the porch steps.
The young man steps out onto the porch, calling, "Hey, lady."
He walks to the edge of the porch and adds, "Hey, wait up."
The young man jogs down the steps toward Phoebe, who stops on the sidewalk near a yellow car. The video ends.
Outside on a sidewalk near a yellow car, Frank Jr. and Phoebe stand facing each other, a house with a white picket fence visible behind them.
Frank Jr. asks, "How do you know my dad?"
Phoebe lowers her gaze slightly.
Phoebe looks up and replies, "Well, I don't really."
Phoebe tilts her head slightly.
Phoebe adds, "Just genetically."
Frank Jr. looks at her. Phoebe crosses her arms.
Phoebe says, "Kind of. My dad too." Frank Jr. exhales and says, "Heavy."
Phoebe says, "Yeah."
Frank Jr. looks down, then back at her.
Frank Jr. asks, "So, did he ever talk about me? Phoebe?"
Phoebe replies, "No. But he didn't really talk about oh," her volume dropping as her voice trails off.
Phoebe shifts her gaze back and forth.
Phoebe finishes, "except stilts."
Frank Jr.'s eyebrows raise. Phoebe nods.
Frank Jr. says, "Stilts? Yeah, he loves stilts. There's one time I was upstairs, I was stealing cigarettes out of my mom's purse,"
Frank Jr. continues, "and all of a sudden, I look over there's, my dad's head bobbing past the window," gesturing a bobbing motion with his hand.
Frank Jr. smiles and repeats the bobbing gesture with both hands.
Frank Jr. smiles widely, demonstrating a wave, and says, "He just had this big smile on his face, and he was waving because he was always happiest when he was on his stilts."
Phoebe smiles slightly, then lowers her gaze.
Phoebe says, "Yeah."
Phoebe looks back at Frank Jr.
Phoebe says, "I don't know what to do with that." Frank Jr. replies, "Me, neither."
Frank Jr. steps toward Phoebe, looks around briefly, and chuckles. They both smile.
Frank Jr. points to Phoebe and says, "So, you're like my big sister."
Phoebe nods and says, "Yeah."
Frank Jr. smiles, pointing to his chest, and says, "This is huge. You can buy me beer."
Phoebe shakes her head, smiling.
Phoebe says, "I'm not gonna but you know what's cool, though? Okay, if you had a friend named Pete, then I could say," gesturing outward with her hands.
Phoebe continues, "oh, yeah, no, I know Pete. He's friends with my brother."
Frank Jr. maintains his gaze on her.
Frank Jr. says, "I got a friend named Mark."
Phoebe smiles and says, "That'll work, too."
Frank Jr. smiles.
Frank Jr. says, "Cool. All right, so maybe"
Frank Jr. tilts his head slightly.
Frank Jr. continues, "I could give you a call sometime? We could talk or something?"
Phoebe smiles at him.
Phoebe says, "Yeah, that'd be okay. All right. Okay."
Frank Jr. says, "I'm in the book." Phoebe says, "Okay. Yeah."
Frank Jr. says, "All right."
Phoebe looks down at her hands, then back up at Frank Jr.
Phoebe asks, "So, stilts?" Frank Jr. replies, "Yeah."
Frank Jr. points his thumb over his shoulder and says, "Hey, if you want, I can take you around back and show you where he hit his head on the rain gutter."
Phoebe smiles and says, "Okay."
Frank Jr. turns and walks toward the house, followed closely by Phoebe.
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Ross and Chandler sit side-by-side on a sofa. Ross holds money and says, "Well, we did it. We're here."
Ross looks at Chandler and adds, "We are standing our ground."
Chandler looks around the room.
Ross looks toward the counter and asks, "How long does a cup of coffee take?"
Chandler leans forward, claps his hands together loudly, and shouts, "Would you come on? Come on."
A waitress approaches holding a tray with two large mugs.
Chandler takes a mug, saying, "Thank you." Ross takes the other mug.
Ross picks up a metal shaker, pouring its contents into his mug and stirring it, while Chandler brings his mug to his mouth.
Ross and Chandler simultaneously lift their mugs and take continuous large gulps of coffee.
They lower their mugs abruptly, both exhaling sharply with their mouths open, their facial muscles tense.
Ross gestures forward with one hand and says, "We go. I think we proved our point."
Chandler nods rapidly.
Ross stands, speaking quickly, "You burn your mouth." Chandler stands and moves toward the door, saying rapidly, "Cannot feel my tongue."
The setting changes to the sidewalk outside the coffee shop. Chandler pushes through the door and onto the sidewalk, followed closely by Ross, as Arthur and Carl stand nearby.
Chandler looks at Arthur and Carl, saying, "Bullies. Big bullies."
Arthur points at Chandler and Ross.
Arthur says, "Look who's here. It's the weenies."
Carl steps forward, his hands clasped in front of him.
Carl asks, "Did we not make ourselves clear the other day?" Ross points to the ground, replying, "Yes. And that's why we're here. Yes. We're standing our ground."
Chandler stares at Arthur.
Chandler says, "Apparently."
Carl looks at Arthur.
Carl adjusts his jacket and says, "Let's do this. All right."
Ross extends his hand. Arthur points at Ross's wrist, saying, "What do you got there? A weapon. It's a nice watch. I don't want to break it on your ribs." Carl adds, "All right, let's do this."
Chandler raises his hands slightly, palms open.
Chandler asks, "Question if I don't care about my watch, can I use it as a weapon?"
Carl asks, "What do you mean?" Chandler raises both fists, replying, "Well, it's sharp. It's metal. I think I can do some serious damage with it."
Arthur gestures with open hands and says, "No, you can't use your watch"
Arthur points at Chandler.
Arthur adds, "or your keys."
Carl looks at Chandler.
Carl says, "Look, here's what we'll do."
Carl gestures toward a blue newspaper box and says, "We'll put all keys and watches in the hat over there. All right?" as he takes off his cap.
They gather around the blue box. Someone says, "All right," as they place items into the cap on the box.
Arthur shoves Ross backward, saying, "Come on, man. Let's do this." Ross raises a finger, asking, "Before I forget, are we hitting faces?"

Arthur raises his fists, asking loudly, "Of course you're hitting faces. Why wouldn't you hit faces?" Ross points to himself, speaking rapidly, "Well, because I have to work on Monday. I have a big presentation"
Carl looks at Ross. The video concludes.
Ross and Chandler stand facing Arthur and Carl on a city street at night. Ross gestures with his hands.
Chandler holds his hands up and says, "I got to show this apartment tomorrow. And this no faces thing may not be a bad idea."
Carl gestures toward his neck and head.
Carl points upward and says, "Nothing from the neck up"
Arthur points downward.
"or the waist down," Arthur adds.
Carl gestures with his hands while speaking to Arthur.
Carl says, "Deena is ovulating." Arthur replies, "Oh, really? You guys trying again?" Carl nods and answers, "Yeah."
Chandler steps forward, pointing to his chest.

"So let me just get this straight. So we're strictly talking about the middle?" Chandler asks. Carl steps forward with raised fists, saying, "Come on." Ross raises his fists, bouncing lightly, and says loudly, "Hey, whoa. You want some of this? You want a piece of"
Ross continues bouncing lightly with his fists raised.
Ross continues, "this standing here?" while raising his hands.
Carl looks past Ross and Chandler toward the background.
Carl points off-screen and yells, "Hey, those guys are taking our stuff."

The four men turn and run down the street, chasing two figures who flee with their belongings. The setting changes to the interior of Central Perk, where a woman sits at a table near the open door. Chandler runs through the doorway, his breathing heavy, and rests his hands on his knees.
Chandler looks up, out of breath, and says, "God, that was amazing."
Ross enters behind Chandler, also out of breath. "That was incredible," he says.
Arthur steps into the doorway and says, "You guys kicked butt."
Ross gestures toward Arthur and Carl, who enters behind Arthur. "US? What about you guys, man?"

Arthur mimics a punch and says, "You really, Bing, gave it to old Mr. Clean back there." Ross nods, "He was a big guy." Arthur says, "Yeah, he was, wasn't he?" Chandler shakes his head, looking down. "I wouldn't know, having missed"
Chandler continues, "everything." Carl points at Chandler and says, "Don't do that to yourself."
Carl gestures with his hand and adds, "Any one of us could have tripped over that little girl's jump rope."
Chandler briefly closes his eyes and smiles slightly, looking down. Ross glances at him from his seat on the couch.
Ross looks toward Arthur and Carl, gesturing with his hands. "So listen, guys."
Ross looks between Arthur and Carl as they sit near him.
Ross asks, "Are we okay here?"
Carl and Arthur look at each other.
Carl nods and says, "We're okay." Chandler, smiling, gestures toward Arthur and asks, "So can I have my hat back?"
Arthur stares back at Chandler, his expression unmoving.
"No," Arthur replies.
Chandler lowers his gaze and presses his lips together. He lunges forward, snatches the cap from Arthur's hands, and runs out the door.
Arthur and Carl chase after Chandler, followed by Ross. The setting changes to a diner with bright neon signs and black-and-white tiled floors. Executive producer credits appear.
Patrons sit at tables and booths. A man in a red sequined jumpsuit and an afro wig walks past the booths.
A blonde woman sitting at a booth with Joey, Chandler, Ross, and another woman points toward the counter and says, "Look at her."
Monica, wearing a voluminous blonde wig and glasses, stands behind the counter, reading a receipt.
Ross waves from the booth and says, "Hi, Monica."
Monica gasps, her mouth dropping open.
The blonde woman at the booth smiles and says, "They going."
Joey smiles, points at Monica, and says, "Hey, nice boo"
Monica presses her lips together and places the receipt on the counter.
Chandler presses buttons on a small tabletop jukebox and says, "sketch. Take a second"
An upbeat song with a male vocalist begins playing. Diner staff and the man in the red jumpsuit step onto the counter.
The staff on the counter perform a choreographed dance. On-screen text lists associate producers.
The choreographed dancing on the counter continues as executive story editor credits appear on screen.
The male vocalist sings. The subtitle reads, "there's?"
The dancing continues on the counter.
The singer sings, "man. Pick yourself up. The ground I said your name and excellent." The staff continue dancing. Guest starring credits appear on screen.
Joey, Chandler, Ross, and the two women watch the performance from their booth, smiling and laughing. Co-starring credits are displayed.
The staff continue dancing on the counter in synchronization to the song. Crew credits appear.
The dancers form the letters "Y", "M", "C", and "A" with their arms as the singer belts out, "It's fun to stay at the YMCA it's fun to stay at the YMCA". Credits continue to roll.
The dancers repeat the arm motions.
The song lyrics conclude with, "YMCA."
The patrons in the diner cheer and clap. The staff remain standing on the counter.
Final production credits appear on screen. The people on the counter resume dancing as the video ends.
